---

<objective>
Calibrate FlawChess endgame thresholds against `reports/benchmarks-2026-05-01.md`:
tighten the clock-pressure neutral band to ±5pp, widen the recovery gauge to
[0.25, 0.40], replace the per-type WDL grouped bar chart with six per-class
mini-gauge cards driven by class-specific Conversion / Recovery bands, delete
the now-redundant Win Rate by Endgame Type table and timeline, and reframe the
endgame insights LLM prompt around delta-from-class-baseline (v18) instead of
absolute per-class win rates.

Purpose: pooled benchmark data shows the prior thresholds were miscalibrated
(±10pp clock-pressure neutral hides ~half of meaningful clock advantages; the
narrow recovery band misclassified typical values as "strong"). Per-class
baselines are required because queen vs minor-piece endgames have inherently
different conversion/recovery distributions — comparing the user against a
single shared band tells the wrong story.

Output: regenerated `endgameZones.ts`, replacement chart component, deleted
WDL/timeline charts, bumped LLM prompt (v18) with per-class baseline framing,
CHANGELOG entry. All changes STAGED ONLY (no commits — per CLAUDE.md "When
working on the main branch with /gsd:quick, don't commit unless explicitly
asked").
</objective>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Update Python zone registry, regenerate TS, replace per-class chart, delete WDL/timeline</name>
  <files>
    app/services/endgame_zones.py
    scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py
    frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts
    frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameConvRecovChart.tsx
    frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameWDLChart.tsx
    frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameTimelineChart.tsx
    frontend/src/components/charts/__tests__/EndgamePerformanceSection.test.tsx
    frontend/src/pages/Endgames.tsx
  </files>
  <action>
1. **Edit `app/services/endgame_zones.py`:**
   - Change `NEUTRAL_PCT_THRESHOLD: float = 10.0` → `5.0`. Update the
     trailing comment to reference `reports/benchmarks-2026-05-01.md`
     (pooled IQR) and `D-XX` style: keep it terse.
   - In `BUCKETED_ZONE_REGISTRY["recovery_save_pct"]`, change the three
     entries from `ZoneSpec(0.25, 0.35, "higher_is_better")` to
     `ZoneSpec(0.25, 0.40, "higher_is_better")`. Update the inline comment
     at lines 218-221 to reflect the new pooled p25/p75 source (no longer
     "median 0.32 / re-centered" — it is now widened, not re-centered).
   - **Add** a new module-level constant `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES` keyed by
     `EndgameClass` literal with the six entries from `<benchmark_values>`.
     Use a typed mapping; declare a small `@dataclass(frozen=True)` if
     useful (e.g. `class PerClassBands` with `conversion: tuple[float, float]`
     and `recovery: tuple[float, float]`) OR a nested dict shape — choose
     whichever produces the cleaner ty-checked output. Add a docstring
     citing `reports/benchmarks-2026-05-01.md` as the source.
   - Import `EndgameClass` from `app.schemas.endgames` (verify the symbol
     exists; if not, use the `Literal[...]` type alias from `app/types/endgames.py`
     or wherever the canonical `EndgameClass` type lives — `grep -rn "EndgameClass" app/` to locate).

2. **Edit `scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py`:**
   - Import the new `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES` constant.
   - Extend `_render()` to emit a new TypeScript export:
     ```ts
     export const PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES = {
       rook:        { conversion: [0.65, 0.75], recovery: [0.28, 0.38] },
       minor_piece: { conversion: [0.63, 0.73], recovery: [0.31, 0.41] },
       pawn:        { conversion: [0.67, 0.77], recovery: [0.26, 0.36] },
       queen:       { conversion: [0.73, 0.83], recovery: [0.20, 0.30] },
       mixed:       { conversion: [0.65, 0.75], recovery: [0.28, 0.38] },
       pawnless:    { conversion: [0.70, 0.80], recovery: [0.21, 0.31] },
     } as const;
     ```
     Bands are `[from, to]` tuples — wrap with `colorizeGaugeZones` on the
     consumer side, same as `FIXED_GAUGE_ZONES`. Add a TS-side comment that
     these are class-specific typical bands for Conversion and Recovery.
   - Also export a TS literal type for the keys
     (`export type EndgameClassKey = keyof typeof PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES;`)
     so consumers can narrow `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` lookups cleanly.

3. **Regenerate the TS mirror:**
   ```
   uv run python scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py
   ```
   Verify with `git diff frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts` that the
   three intended changes (NEUTRAL_PCT_THRESHOLD=5.0, recovery band 0.40,
   PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES export) are present.

4. **Replace `frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameConvRecovChart.tsx`:**
   - Drop the recharts BarChart entirely.
   - Render a responsive grid:
     ```tsx
     <div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4"
          data-testid="conv-recov-grid">
     ```
   - For each `cat` in `categories` (filter same as before:
     `conversion_games > 0 || recovery_games > 0`), render a card:
     ```tsx
     <div
       className="rounded border border-border p-4 space-y-3"
       data-testid={`class-gauge-${cat.endgame_class}`}
     >
       <div className="flex items-baseline justify-between">
         <h4 className="text-sm font-medium">{cat.label}</h4>
         {/* Sparse-cohort caveat for pawnless or any cat with both
             {conversion,recovery}_games < MIN_GAMES_FOR_RELIABLE_STATS */}
         {(cat.conversion.conversion_games + cat.conversion.recovery_games)
            < MIN_GAMES_FOR_RELIABLE_STATS && (
           <span className="text-xs text-muted-foreground tabular-nums">
             n={cat.conversion.conversion_games + cat.conversion.recovery_games}
           </span>
         )}
       </div>
       <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2">
         <EndgameGauge
           value={cat.conversion.conversion_pct}
           maxValue={100}
           label="Conversion"
           zones={colorizeGaugeZones([
             { from: 0,    to: convLower },
             { from: convLower, to: convUpper },
             { from: convUpper, to: 1.0 },
           ])}
         />
         <EndgameGauge ... recovery same shape ... />
       </div>
     </div>
     ```
     where `convLower, convUpper` come from
     `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES[cat.endgame_class].conversion[0/1]` (narrow with
     a `const bands = PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES[cat.endgame_class]; if (!bands) return null;`
     guard for `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`).
   - Imports: `EndgameGauge`, `colorizeGaugeZones`, `MIN_GAMES_FOR_RELIABLE_STATS`,
     `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES`.
   - Keep the section heading + InfoPopover from the existing component
     (update the InfoPopover text to describe per-class baselines instead
     of grouped bars).
   - Update the empty-state branch (no categories with games) to still
     render a sensible message.
   - Remove the now-unused recharts imports and `chartConfig`.
   - Remove the `MOBILE_BREAKPOINT_PX` / `useIsMobile` hook from this file
     if grid breakpoints alone handle responsiveness.

5. **Delete `frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameWDLChart.tsx`** (whole file).

6. **Delete `frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameTimelineChart.tsx`** (whole file).

7. **Edit `frontend/src/pages/Endgames.tsx`:**
   - Remove the imports at lines ~19, ~22:
     `import { EndgameWDLChart }` and `import { EndgameTimelineChart }`.
   - Locate the "Endgame Type Breakdown" section (line ~432-450). Replace
     the WDL chart wrapper + the timeline wrapper with just the
     ConvRecovChart wrapper. Keep the section heading and the
     `<SectionInsightSlot sectionId="type_breakdown" ...>` slot. Final
     section should look roughly:
     ```tsx
     <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-foreground mt-2">
       Endgame Type Breakdown
     </h2>
     {statsData.categories.length > 0 && (
       <div className="charcoal-texture rounded-md p-4">
         <EndgameConvRecovChart categories={statsData.categories} />
       </div>
     )}
     <SectionInsightSlot sectionId="type_breakdown" data={sectionBySection.type_breakdown} />
     ```
   - Remove any now-orphaned references to `handleCategorySelect`,
     `timelineData`, `showTimeline` if they were used ONLY by the deleted
     charts (grep within the file before deleting — `handleCategorySelect`
     is also used by the Games tab dropdown so keep it).
   - Run `npm run lint` and `npm run knip` after editing to confirm no
     dead exports or unused imports remain.

8. **Update `frontend/src/components/charts/__tests__/EndgamePerformanceSection.test.tsx`:**
   - If the test imports anything from EndgameWDLChart or EndgameTimelineChart,
     remove those imports and any related assertions.
   - If the test renders the parent layout and asserts on type-breakdown
     content, update assertions to expect `data-testid="conv-recov-grid"`
     and `data-testid="class-gauge-rook"` (etc.) instead of the deleted
     `endgame-wdl-chart` / `endgame-type-table` testids.
   - If the existing assertions are entirely unrelated, leave them alone.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>uv run python scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py &amp;&amp; git diff --exit-code frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts || echo "REGEN OK"; uv run ruff check . &amp;&amp; uv run ty check app/ tests/ &amp;&amp; uv run pytest -x app/services/test_endgame_zones.py 2>/dev/null || uv run pytest -x tests/services/test_endgame_zones.py 2>/dev/null; cd frontend &amp;&amp; npm run lint &amp;&amp; npm run knip &amp;&amp; npm test -- --run; cd ..</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - `app/services/endgame_zones.py` shows `NEUTRAL_PCT_THRESHOLD: float = 5.0`,
      recovery band `[0.25, 0.40]`, and a new `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES` mapping

    - `frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts` (regenerated) shows the same

    - `EndgameConvRecovChart.tsx` renders six per-class mini-gauge cards in a

      `data-testid="class-gauge-{class}"`, and the parent grid has
      `data-testid="conv-recov-grid"`.
    - `EndgameWDLChart.tsx` and `EndgameTimelineChart.tsx` no longer exist.
    - `frontend/src/pages/Endgames.tsx` no longer imports them and the
      Endgame Type Breakdown section renders only the per-class mini-gauges
      and the section-insight slot.
    - `npm run lint`, `npm run knip`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` all
      pass with no dead-export or missing-testid complaints.
    - `uv run ruff check . && uv run ty check app/ tests/ && uv run pytest`
      all pass.
  </done>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Bump LLM prompt to v18 — drop win-rate-by-type framing, add per-class baseline payload</name>
  <files>
    app/services/insights_llm.py
    app/services/insights_service.py
    app/prompts/endgame_insights.md
  </files>
  <action>
1. **Bump prompt version in `app/services/insights_llm.py`:**
   - Locate `_PROMPT_VERSION = "endgame_v17"` at line 61.
   - Change to `_PROMPT_VERSION = "endgame_v18"`. Append a v18 changelog
     entry to the trailing comment (one sentence) summarizing:
     "v18 (260501-s0u benchmark calibration v2): drop per-class win_rate
     framing in favor of per-class delta-from-baseline. Conversion / Recovery
     are now narrated against class-specific typical bands sourced from
     `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES` (reports/benchmarks-2026-05-01.md). Added
     `class_baselines` block to the user-prompt payload; removed the
     `Win Rate by Endgame Type` chart caption. type_win_rate_timeline
     subsection deprecated — no longer rendered."

2. **Reframe per-class payload in `app/services/insights_llm.py`:**
   - Locate the formatter that emits the `## Chart: results_by_endgame_type_wdl`
     block and the per-class lines (search for `endgame_class:<13` formatting
     around line 562 / 577).
   - Drop the `win_pct / draw_pct / loss_pct / score_pct` row format for
     per-class rows. Replace with a per-class block of the shape:
     ```
     ## Per-class performance vs class baseline
     | class       | conv_pct | conv_baseline_mid | conv_delta | recov_pct | recov_baseline_mid | recov_delta | n_seq |
     | ----------- | -------- | ----------------- | ---------- | --------- | ------------------ | ----------- | ----- |
     | rook        |    72.3% |             70.0% |      +2.3% |     31.0% |              33.0% |       -2.0% |  483  |
     ...
     ```
     Compute baseline midpoints from
     `(PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES[class].conversion[0] + [1]) / 2` etc. — load
     these from the Python registry directly (see Task 1 step 1's
     `PER_CLASS_GAUGE_ZONES` constant). Sparse classes (n < 10) keep the
     `(low)` annotation suffix.
   - For the `[asymmetry type=...]` and `[weakest-type]` payload markers
     emitted upstream, keep them but change the underlying citation values
     from `score_pct` to `conv_delta` / `recov_delta` where the prompt body
     references them. (See step 4 below.)

3. **Trim findings emitter in `app/services/insights_service.py`:**
   - `_findings_results_by_endgame_type` (line ~919) currently emits
     `win_rate` SubsectionFinding rows. Replace with two findings per class:
     one for `conversion_win_pct` (bucket-less; pass the per-class band to
     `assign_zone` via a new helper) and one for `recovery_save_pct`. If
     the cleanest approach is a new `assign_per_class_zone(metric_id, klass, value)`
     helper in `endgame_zones.py`, add it there (single source of truth for
     band lookups).
   - `_findings_type_win_rate_timeline` (line ~1068): drop the function
     entirely, along with its registration in
     `insights_service.py` line ~391 (`findings.extend(_findings_type_win_rate_timeline(...))`).
     Also remove the subsection from the `_TIMELINE_SUBSECTION_IDS` frozenset
     in `insights_llm.py` (line ~300-307) and from the `SubsectionId` Literal
     in `app/services/endgame_zones.py` (line ~62) — the trailing
     `"type_win_rate_timeline"` literal entry can be removed since no code
     emits findings for it anymore.
   - `SAMPLE_QUALITY_BANDS["type_win_rate_timeline"]` in
     `app/services/endgame_zones.py` line ~246 — remove that entry too
     (and the `SubsectionId` Literal trim above keeps Mapping coverage
     exhaustive).
   - Run `uv run ty check app/ tests/` after this trim — ty will flag any
     remaining string literal that is no longer a member of `SubsectionId`.

4. **Update `app/prompts/endgame_insights.md`:**
   - In the UI vocabulary table (around line 81): remove the
     `win_rate (per type)` row. Replace with `conv_delta (per type)` and
     `recov_delta (per type)` rows describing them as
     "user's Conversion / Recovery rate minus the class baseline midpoint".
   - Remove the `win_rate citation rule` paragraph at line ~85 (no longer
     emitted).
   - Around line ~125: remove `type_win_rate_timeline` from the timeline
     subsections list.
   - Around line ~330: remove the `win_rate (per endgame type)` glossary
     bullet. Replace with a `conv_delta` / `recov_delta` glossary entry
     citing the per-class baseline source.
   - Around lines ~348-357: drop the rows referencing
     `type_win_rate_timeline` and `Chart: results_by_endgame_type_wdl`. The
     replacement subsection name is `conversion_recovery_by_type` (already
     listed) — re-route any rules that previously pointed at the WDL chart
     to point at the per-class delta block created in step 2.
   - In the "Intra-type asymmetry story" section (line ~246): the
     `[asymmetry type=...]` framing still applies, but reword from
     "leading with `score_pct`" to "leading with the larger absolute
     `conv_delta` or `recov_delta`".
   - Add a note near the top of the `type_breakdown` section guidance:
     "Per-class baselines differ by endgame class — Queen conversion
     typically runs ~78% (the typical band is [73%, 83%]) while
     minor-piece recovery typically runs ~36% (band [31%, 41%]). Always
     interpret user values as deltas from these class-specific bands, not
     against a universal threshold."
   - Add a one-line caveat about pawnless: "pawnless is sparse — note when
     n is small."
   - Increment any prompt-internal version markers if present.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>uv run ruff check . &amp;&amp; uv run ruff format --check . &amp;&amp; uv run ty check app/ tests/ &amp;&amp; uv run pytest -x</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - `_PROMPT_VERSION` is `"endgame_v18"` with a v18 changelog comment.
    - The user-prompt payload no longer contains per-class
      `win_pct / draw_pct / loss_pct / score_pct` rows; instead it emits a
      per-class block with user `conv_pct`, user `recov_pct`, baseline
      midpoints, and signed deltas.
    - `_findings_type_win_rate_timeline` is gone; `_findings_results_by_endgame_type`
      now emits per-class conversion + recovery deltas (or, if the executor
      prefers, a per-class composite finding).
    - `type_win_rate_timeline` is gone from `_TIMELINE_SUBSECTION_IDS`,
      `SubsectionId` Literal, and `SAMPLE_QUALITY_BANDS`.
    - `app/prompts/endgame_insights.md` no longer references
      `Win Rate by Endgame Type`, `type_win_rate_timeline`, or the
      `win_rate citation rule`. The `type_breakdown` guidance points at
      per-class baselines.
    - `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run ty check app/ tests/ && uv run pytest` all pass.
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 3: CHANGELOG entry, full verification suite, stage everything (no commit)</name>
  <files>
    CHANGELOG.md
  </files>
  <action>
1. **Append to `CHANGELOG.md`** under the existing `## [Unreleased]` heading
   (do not create a new heading; do not move anything to a versioned
   section). Add or extend the existing `### Changed` and `### Removed`
   subsections so the new entries cluster correctly:

   ```
   ### Changed
   - Calibrate clock-pressure neutral band to ±5pp (was ±10) — pooled IQR from benchmarks-2026-05-01
   - Widen recovery gauge band to [0.25, 0.40] — pooled p25/p75
   - Per-class neutral bands for conversion and recovery in endgame type breakdown
   - Endgame insights LLM prompt reframed around per-class baselines (v18)

   ### Removed
   - "Win Rate by Endgame Type" section and timeline (signal too weak across classes — pooled per-class score-diffs all in [+0.032, +0.049])
   ```

   If `### Changed` / `### Removed` already exist under `[Unreleased]`,
   append the new bullets at the bottom of the matching subsection rather
   than duplicating the heading.

2. **Run the full verification suite** (the executor must run every
   command listed below; failure of any single check is a hard stop —
   diagnose and fix before staging):

   ```
   # Backend
   uv run ruff check .
   uv run ruff format --check .
   uv run ty check app/ tests/
   uv run pytest

   # Frontend (run from repo root with `cd frontend`)
   (cd frontend && npm run lint)
   (cd frontend && npm run knip)
   (cd frontend && npm test -- --run)
   (cd frontend && npm run build)

   # Codegen drift guard (CI runs this and blocks the build on drift)
   uv run python scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py
   git diff --exit-code frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts
   ```

3. **Stage everything (DO NOT COMMIT):**
   ```
   git add \
     app/services/endgame_zones.py \
     app/services/insights_llm.py \
     app/services/insights_service.py \
     app/prompts/endgame_insights.md \
     scripts/gen_endgame_zones_ts.py \
     frontend/src/generated/endgameZones.ts \
     frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameConvRecovChart.tsx \
     frontend/src/pages/Endgames.tsx \
     frontend/src/components/charts/__tests__/EndgamePerformanceSection.test.tsx \
     CHANGELOG.md
   git rm \
     frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameWDLChart.tsx \
     frontend/src/components/charts/EndgameTimelineChart.tsx
   ```
   Then run `git status` and `git diff --staged --stat` and capture the
   summary for the orchestrator's SUMMARY.md.

4. **Manual smoke-test reminder (NOT executed by the agent — for SUMMARY):**
   In SUMMARY.md note that the user should manually:
   - Load the Endgames page and verify six mini-gauge cards render
     (rook / minor_piece / pawn / queen / mixed / pawnless).
   - Confirm the "Win Rate by Endgame Type" section and the per-type
     timeline are gone.
   - Resize to mobile width and confirm the grid collapses cleanly to a
     single column.
   - Trigger an endgame-insights regeneration (or wait for the next cache
     miss) and confirm the LLM narration leads with class-baseline framing
     instead of absolute win rates.
  </action>

  </verify>
  <done>
